_description: Controlled vocabulary for person-level and situation-level dimensions used to parameterize mechanism activation without encoding demographic stereotypes. Person dimensions reference validated psychological constructs; situation dimensions reference features of the environment.

person_trait_dimensions._note: Stable individual-level constructs. Reference these in person_moderators.
person_trait_dimensions.big_five_O: Openness to experience (Big Five) — curiosity, creativity, cognitive flexibility
person_trait_dimensions.big_five_C: Conscientiousness (Big Five) — self-control, planning, goal persistence
person_trait_dimensions.big_five_E: Extraversion (Big Five) — sociability, positive affect, dominance
person_trait_dimensions.big_five_A: Agreeableness (Big Five) — cooperation, trust, empathy orientation
person_trait_dimensions.big_five_N: Neuroticism (Big Five) — emotional instability, threat sensitivity
person_trait_dimensions.dark_triad_machiavellianism: Machiavellianism — strategic manipulation, low affective empathy
person_trait_dimensions.attachment_anxious: Anxious attachment — fear of abandonment, hyperactivation of attachment system
person_trait_dimensions.attachment_avoidant: Avoidant attachment — deactivation of attachment, discomfort with intimacy
person_trait_dimensions.bis_sensitivity: Behavioral Inhibition System sensitivity — threat/punishment sensitivity, behavioral suppression
person_trait_dimensions.bas_sensitivity: Behavioral Activation System sensitivity — reward sensitivity, approach motivation
person_trait_dimensions.need_for_cognition: Intrinsic motivation to engage in effortful thinking
person_trait_dimensions.need_for_closure: Desire for definite answers, intolerance of ambiguity
person_trait_dimensions.disgust_sensitivity: Sensitivity to contamination and violations of purity norms
person_trait_dimensions.sensory_processing_sensitivity: Depth of processing, emotional reactivity, environmental sensitivity (HSP)
person_trait_dimensions.alexithymia: Difficulty identifying and describing emotions — low emotional granularity
person_trait_dimensions.social_dominance_orientation: Preference for group-based hierarchy and inequality
person_trait_dimensions.hexaco_H: Honesty-Humility (HEXACO sixth factor) — sincerity, fairness, greed-avoidance, modesty; predicts dark triad behaviors and moral licensing where Big Five falls short

person_state_dimensions._note: Transient within-person states. Reference these in person_moderators for state-dependent activation.
person_state_dimensions.cognitive_load: Current mental load — high load degrades deliberate reasoning, amplifies System 1
person_state_dimensions.affective_valence: Current emotional tone — positive or negative affect baseline
person_state_dimensions.affective_arousal: Current physiological arousal level — amplifies dominant response tendencies
person_state_dimensions.resource_scarcity_state: Perceived scarcity of resources (money, time, social support) — tunnels cognition
person_state_dimensions.power_state: Perceived current power level — affects perspective-taking, approach/inhibition balance
person_state_dimensions.threat_appraisal: Degree to which current environment is appraised as threatening
person_state_dimensions.in_group_salience: How activated is social identity / group membership in this context
person_state_dimensions.relationship_history: Quality and valence of prior interactions with the other party
person_state_dimensions.fatigue_depletion: Current self-regulatory resource depletion — increases impulsive responding

situation_dimensions._note: Features of the environment/context. Reference these in situation_activators.
situation_dimensions.social_visibility: Whether behavior is observable by others — private/semi-public/public
situation_dimensions.ambiguity: Degree of uncertainty about situation, others' intentions, or correct response
situation_dimensions.out_group_salience: Whether out-group members are present or psychologically salient
situation_dimensions.power_differential: Whether a clear power asymmetry exists between parties
situation_dimensions.power_holder: The profiled person holds the power position in an asymmetric relationship
situation_dimensions.power_low: The profiled person is in the lower-power position in an asymmetric relationship
situation_dimensions.resource_availability: Whether resources (money, status, goods, support) are abundant or scarce
situation_dimensions.novelty: Whether the situation is familiar or novel/unprecedented
situation_dimensions.relationship_type: Nature of relationship: stranger / acquaintance / close / authority / rival
situation_dimensions.conflict_present: Whether explicit conflict, competition, or zero-sum framing is active
situation_dimensions.anonymity: Degree to which individual identity is obscured
situation_dimensions.social_norms_clarity: How clear and enforced the relevant social norms are
situation_dimensions.physical_threat: Whether physical danger is present or implied
situation_dimensions.group_context: Whether the person is acting individually or as part of a group
situation_dimensions.outcome_reversibility: Whether the decision or outcome can be undone
situation_dimensions.prior_commitment: Whether the person has made prior public or private commitments relevant to this situation
situation_dimensions.surveillance: Whether the person believes they are being observed or evaluated

cultural_dimensions._note: Documented cross-cultural moderators with empirical backing. Use only when research specifically identifies cultural moderation. These are population-level tendencies, not individual-level predictors.
cultural_dimensions.individualism_collectivism: Hofstede dimension — extent to which group vs. individual interest is prioritized
cultural_dimensions.uncertainty_avoidance: Tolerance for ambiguity and unstructured situations
cultural_dimensions.tightness_looseness: Strength of social norms and tolerance for deviance (Gelfand)
